zh.title: 雪崩币/美元技术分析：短线弱势震荡偏空
zh.content:
当前AVAX整体维持偏弱震荡结构，价格运行在20日均线6.32下方，短期空头略占优势，但下方仍有买盘支撑未完全破位结构延续  

技术指标方面，MACD持续为负值且柱体收缩，显示下行动能减弱但尚未金叉，短线仍偏空整理结构未完成反转信号确认不足偏弱运行  

支撑阻力方面，下方支撑关注6.14一带及6.10区域，上方阻力位于6.32均线与6.45前高附近构成区间震荡带运行为主  

操作建议以反弹高空为主，若跌破6.14可顺势加速下探，未破前谨慎追空并等待反弹确认控制仓位与风险避免追涨杀跌区间交易思路

en.title: Avalanche vs US Dollar Technical Analysis
en.content:
Avalanche is currently trading at 6.214 with a mild 24h gain of 1.17%, but the broader structure still reflects a corrective phase rather than a sustained bullish reversal. Price action remains tightly compressed between 6.1453 and 6.2159, indicating low volatility and equilibrium after recent downside pressure. Despite the intraday uptick, the market is still trading beneath the 20-day SMA at 6.32580, which suggests the dominant short-term trend remains bearish.

Momentum indicators reinforce this cautious outlook. The MACD reading at -0.0633 is still below the zero line, signaling that bearish momentum has not fully dissipated. Without an RSI value, momentum confirmation is limited, but price behavior relative to the SMA implies continued weakness. The failure to reclaim the moving average suggests that sellers are still defending higher levels, and any upward moves may currently be corrective rather than trend-reversing.

Key support is located at the recent low near 6.1453, which has acted as a short-term floor. A breakdown below this level could expose psychological support around 6.00, followed by a deeper zone near 5.85 if selling accelerates. On the upside, immediate resistance sits at 6.2159, with stronger resistance at the 20-day SMA around 6.32580, which is the critical level to flip for any bullish continuation.

From a trading perspective, conditions favor a cautious, range-bound approach. Short-term traders may look for rejection signals near 6.21–6.32 for potential downside continuation setups, while long positions are better considered only after a decisive reclaim of the SMA20 with volume confirmation. Risk management remains important given the tight range, with stops ideally placed below 6.14 for longs or above 6.33 for breakout attempts.
